frameworks.name: brain-wide distributed coding
frameworks.one_line: task variables represented across many regions rather than localized to specialist areas

frameworks.name: perceptual decision-making
frameworks.one_line: sensory-cognitive-motor task as a substrate for probing region-by-region neural correlates

core_question: How are sensory, cognitive, and motor variables of a decision-making task represented across the whole mouse brain at single-neuron resolution?
key_assumptions: standardized task and recording protocol enables cross-lab pooling
Neuropixels sampling across 279 areas approximates a brain-wide view
task-variable encoding can be read off from single-neuron firing
manipulations: task variables: visual stimulus, choice, motor action, reward
recording site across 279 brain areas in forebrain, midbrain, hindbrain, cerebellum
key_findings: visual-stimulus representations appear transiently in classical visual areas after onset
stimulus signals then spread to ramp-like activity in midbrain and hindbrain regions that also encode choice
impending motor-action correlates appear almost everywhere in the brain
reward delivery and consumption responses are widespread
interpretations: decision-related computations are distributed across many regions rather than confined to specialist areas
the standardized brain-wide dataset is offered as a community resource
